concept: Ecological Community / Co-Occurring Populations in Relation
description: Co-occurring populations considered in relation to each other. An ecological community holds predation and disease as readily as mutual support, and plenty of its organisms never directly meet; it is a different thing from 'sila', the human community.
sound_symbolism: Living 'li' opens into the complete mutual form 'weso', letting several populations meet without merging their differences. The rounded ending suggests coexistence while leaving competition, predation, asymmetry, and absence equally speakable.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha liweso lo liloni lorea.' (This ecological community connects populations.) State the selected place, time, populations, and relation where ambiguity matters. Distinct from 'sila' (human community), 'phaliso' (network), 'litero' (ecosystem), and a simple list of species.

pillars.solarpunk-values: The word makes multispecies consequences available to community decisions without assuming that ecological relations are peaceful or self-balancing.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Different living forms are understood through their patterned coexistence, while aesthetic interweaving remains distinct from ecological function.

tags.nature: several living populations considered together in ecological relation
tags.spatial: co-occurrence within a selected place or distributed area
